zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Abraham於1515年出生在英國時，他的父親Richard Abram18歲，母親Margaret20歲。他於1541年與Judith Woodward在英國結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。他去世於1623年3月20日在他的家鄉享年108歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Abraham于1515年出生在英国时，他的父亲Richard Abram18岁，母亲Margaret20岁。他于1541年与Judith Woodward在英国结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。他去世于1623年3月20日在他的家乡享年108岁，埋葬在英国英格兰諾薩普頓史爾利。

nameMeanings.name: Abraham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Mathai, Mohan, Raju, Saji, Prasad, Anil, Babu, Biju, Leela, Sajan, Shibu, Suma.

English, French, Spanish, German, Slovenian, Ethiopian, and Assyrian/Chaldean; Hungarian (Ábrahám), Slovak (mainly Abrahám, also Ábrahám), Czech (also Abrahám): from the Hebrew personal name ʾAbraham (modern spelling Avraham), borne by the Biblical patriarch revered by Jews as the founding father of the Jewish people (Genesis 11–25) and by Muslims as founder of all the Semitic peoples, both Hebrew and Arab (compare Ibrahim ). The original name of the Biblical patriarch was probably Abram , meaning ‘high father’ (from ab father, ram ‘exalted’), while its later form is explained in Genesis 17:5 as being derived from Hebrew ab hamon goyim ‘father of a multitude of nations’. It was widely used as a personal name among Christians as well as Jews in the Middle Ages. The name Abraham is also found among Christians in southern India, but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. As a surname of Hungarian origin Abraham is also found in Romania. — Note: Since Ethiopians do not have hereditary surnames, the Ethiopian name was registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.
Irish: adoption of the English name (see 1 above) as an equivalent of Gaelic Mac an Bhreitheamhan ‘son of the judge’. See McBroom and compare Breheny .

variants.name: Abrahams
meanings: English, Dutch, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): patronymic from the personal name   Abraham  .

variants.name: Ibrahim
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Ibrahim, Mohamed, Hassan, Ahmed, Mohammad, Ashraf, Ali, Samir, Ismail, Mohammed, Abdul, Ahmad.

Arabic: from the personal name Ibrāhīm, Arabic form of Abraham . In Islam, Ibrāhīm is identified as a prophet, the ancestor of all the Semitic peoples, both Hebrew and Arab, and the father of Ismāʿīl (see Ismail ) and Isḥāq (see Ishaq ). Bearers of this surname are both Muslims and Christians. Compare Ebrahim .

variants.name: Abrahamson
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Aagot, Algot, Anders, Eskil, Lars, Thora.
Americanized form (and a rare Swedish variant) of Swedish Abrahamsson: patronymic from the personal name Abraham .
Americanized form of Norwegian, Danish, and North German   Abrahamsen  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Abrajan
meanings: Hispanic (mainly Mexico): variant of   Abraham  .

variants.name: Abrahamsen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Alf, Oddvar, Sven, Thor, Lars, Lief, Per.
Norwegian, Danish, Dutch, and North German: patronymic from the personal name   Abraham  . Compare   Abrahamson  .

variants.name: Abramo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Carmelo, Giuseppe, Guido, Luigi, Nunzio, Sal, Salvator, Saverio, Silvio.
Italian: from the personal name Abramo (see Abraham ).

variants.name: Abeles
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Aryeh, Mort.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): patronymic from Abele, a pet form of the Yiddish personal name Abe (from Hebrew ab ‘father’ or from the personal name Abraham ) + Yiddish possessive -s.
English: variant of Abel with possessive or excrescent -s.

variants.name: Abrahamian
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Armenian Armen, Sarkis, Harmik, Seta, Zaven, Adrine, Ara, Avadis, Bedros, Garnik, Hrach, Hrand.
Armenian: patronymic from the Biblical personal name   Abraham  . It is a variant of eastern Armenian   Abrahamyan   and western Armenian   Aprahamian  , found mainly in the US, Iran, and Lebanon.

variants.name: Abrahamyan
meanings: Armenian: patronymic from the eastern Armenian Biblical personal name   Abraham  . Compare   Abrahamian  ,   Abramyan  , and   Aprahamian  .

variants.name: Abram
meanings: English, German, Dutch, Polish, Slovenian, Croatian, and Jewish; Hungarian (Ábrám): from a shortened form of the Biblical personal name Abraham .

English: habitational name from a place near Manchester, recorded as Adburgham in the 12th century, Abraham in the late 14th century, and Abram in the 15th. The placename is from the Old English female personal name Ēadburg + hām ‘homestead’, meaning ‘Ēadburg's homestead’.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Abraham was born in 1515, in England, his father, Richard Abram, was 18 and his mother, Margaret, was 20. He married Judith Woodward in 1541, in England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. He died on 20 March 1623, in his hometown, at the age of 108, and was buried in Pytchley, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Abraham est né en 1515, à Angleterre, son père, Richard Abram, avait 18 ans et sa mère, Margaret, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Judith Woodward en 1541, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Il est décédé le 20 mars 1623, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 108 ans, et a été enterré à Pytchley, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Abraham 1515 in England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard Abram 18 und seine Mutter Margaret 20. Er hat 1541 in England Judith Woodward geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Er starb am 20. März 1623 im Alter von 108 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Pytchley, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Abraham è nato il nel 1515 nel England, il suo padre, Richard Abram, aveva 18 anni e il suo madre, Margaret, aveva 20 anni. Lui sposato Judith Woodward nel 1541, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Morì il 20 marzo 1623, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 108 anni, e fu sepolto a Pytchley, Northamptonshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Abrahamが1515年にイギリスで生まれた時，父Richard Abramは18歳，母Margaretは20歳であった。1541年にイギリスでJudith Woodwardと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼は1623年3月20日に故郷で108歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアプイトチリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Abraham(이)가 1515년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard Abram(은)는 18세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1541년 영국에서 Judith Woodward(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1623년 3월 20일 그의 고향에서 108세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 p이트클리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Abraham nasceu em 1515, em Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard Abram, tinha 18 anos e sua mãe, Margaret, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Judith Woodward em 1541, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. Ele faleceu em 20 de março de 1623, em sua cidade natal, com 108 anos, e foi sepultado em Pytchley, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Abraham родился в 1515 г. в местности Англия, его отцу по имени Richard Abram было 18 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Judith Woodward в 1541 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Он умер 20 марта 1623 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 108 лет и был похоронен в местности пичли, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Abraham nació en 1515, en Inglaterra, su papá, Richard Abram, tenía 18 años y su mamá, Margaret, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Judith Woodward en 1541, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos. Murió el 20 de marzo de 1623, en su pueblo, a la edad de 108 años, y fue sepultado en Pytchley, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
